introduction:
The Ration Card in Karnataka is issued by the Department of Food, Civil Supplies & Consumer Affairs, Government of Karnataka. It entitles eligible households to receive subsidised food commodities — rice, wheat, ragi, sugar, and kerosene — through the Public Distribution System (PDS) at authorised Fair Price Shops (Raitha Samparka Kendras).

The ration card is also a widely accepted identity and address proof across Karnataka. The state runs flagship welfare schemes like Anna Bhagya (10 kg free rice per person/month for BPL families) and Indira Canteen that are linked to your ration card status.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Applying for a new ration card is FREE of cost online (nominal fee at Grama One / Karnataka One centers)
  • Processing time is approximately 15 to 30 working days
  • You must be a permanent resident of Karnataka to apply
  • Only one ration card per family is allowed
  • Aadhaar linking is mandatory for all family members
  • BPL families get Anna Bhagya benefit: 10 kg free rice per person/month (full in-kind distribution since Feb 2025)
  • Track your application at ahara.karnataka.gov.in \u2192 e-Services \u2192 e-Status
  • Helpline: 1967 (PDS) & 1800-425-9339 (Toll-Free)
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Ration Card

Note: Submit self-attested photocopies at the Nadakacheri / Seva Sindhu center and carry original documents for verification.
Identity Proof (Any one)
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID Card
  • PAN Card
  • Passport
  • Driving Licence
  • MGNREGS Job Card
Address Proof (Any one)
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Electricity Bill (recent)
  • Water / Telephone Bill
  • Bank Passbook
  • Registered Rent Agreement
  • Property Tax Receipt
Income & Family Documents
  • Income Certificate (from Tahsildar / Nadakacheri)
  • Birth Certificates of children
  • Marriage Certificate (if applicable)
  • Passport-size photos of all family members
  • Caste Certificate (if applying for BPL/AAY)
Category Guide
  • AAY (Antyodaya): Poorest of poor — free grain entitlement
  • PHH (Priority Households): BPL families under NFSA
  • NPHH (Non-Priority): APL / above poverty line families

Note: PHH / AAY eligibility requires income certificate from Tahsildar.
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via Ahara Portal (ahara.karnataka.gov.in)

Karnataka offers online ration card applications through the official Ahara portal of the Department of Food, Civil Supplies & Consumer Affairs. You need an Aadhaar-linked mobile number for OTP/biometric authentication.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the Official Portal
    Go to ahara.karnataka.gov.in (also accessible at ahara.kar.nic.in)
  2. Navigate to e-Services
    On the homepage, click on the "e-Services" tab \u2192 select "e-Ration Card" \u2192 click "New Ration Card".
  3. Select Language & Card Type
    Choose your preferred language (Kannada / English). Then select "New Ration Card Request" and pick the card type (Priority Household / Non-Priority / AAY).
  4. Aadhaar Authentication
    Enter the head of family's Aadhaar number and click "Go". Authenticate via OTP sent to your Aadhaar-linked mobile or fingerprint biometric. Your details will auto-populate from Aadhaar.
  5. Add Family Members
    Add each family member by entering their Aadhaar number and completing OTP/biometric authentication for each member individually.
  6. Fill Address & Select FPS
    Select your District, Taluk, Hobli, Village/Ward, and choose the nearest Fair Price Shop (FPS). Enter income details and LPG connection information if applicable.
  7. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned copies of address proof, income certificate, and passport-size photos. File size limit: 1 MB per file (JPG/PDF)
  8. Submit & Save Acknowledgement Number
    Review all details, click Submit. An Acknowledgement Number will be generated. Save this number! Use it to track your application status on the same portal under e-Status.
  9. Field Verification by Food Inspector
    A Food Inspector will visit your home within 15 working days to verify your details and documents.
Tip: Keep all original documents ready at home during the Food Inspector's visit. Ensure all family members' Aadhaar details are correct and mobile numbers are active.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Grama One / Karnataka One / Bangalore One Center

If you cannot apply online, visit any authorized citizen service center \u2014 Grama One (for rural areas), Karnataka One, or Bangalore One (for Bengaluru city). You can also visit Nadakacheri (Atalji Janasnehi Kendra) or Common Service Centres (CSC).

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the Nearest Center
    Locate your nearest Grama One / Karnataka One / Bangalore One center. Working hours: 10:00 AM \u2013 5:30 PM (Mon\u2013Sat, closed on 2nd Saturday & public holidays).
  2. Request Ration Card Application
    Tell the center operator you want to apply for a New Ration Card. Hand over your Aadhaar cards and keep your mobile phone handy for OTP. A nominal service fee (\u20b925 \u2013 \u20b950) is charged by the center.
  3. Aadhaar OTP / Biometric Authentication
    The operator will enter your details on the Ahara portal. You and each family member must authenticate via Aadhaar OTP or fingerprint biometric scanner available at the center.
  4. Provide Documents & Details
    Hand over photocopies of address proof, income certificate, and passport-size photos. The operator will fill in income details, select your preferred Fair Price Shop (FPS), and upload documents on your behalf.
  5. Collect Acknowledgement Receipt
    Once submitted, the operator prints an Acknowledgement Receipt with your Application Number. Keep this safe! Use it to track your application at ahara.karnataka.gov.in.
  6. Field Verification & Card Delivery
    A Food Inspector will verify your application via a home visit. Once approved, you can download the e-Ration Card from the portal, or collect a printed copy from the center.
Processing Time: Approximately 15 to 30 working days from the date of application submission.
afterApplying:
After Applying \u2014 What Next?

Track Your Application

Visit ahara.karnataka.gov.in
Click e-Services \u2192 e-Status \u2192 "Status of new/existing ration card". Select your district and enter your Acknowledgement Number.

Download e-Ration Card

Once approved, download your e-Ration Card from the Ahara portal using your Aadhaar number. The digital copy is valid at all Fair Price Shops (Raitha Samparka Kendras).

Anna Bhagya Scheme

BPL/PHH families receive 10 kg free rice per person/month under Anna Bhagya. Since Feb 2025, the full rice quota is distributed in-kind at your FPS. Ensure your Aadhaar is seeded with your bank account.

Raise a Grievance

If not processed within 30 days, call PDS Helpline: 1967 or Toll-Free: 1800-425-9339, or use the Grievance Redressal section on the Ahara portal.
